verdicts.title: No-rate-currency settle silently fabricates a 1:1 euroValue — corrupts EUR/Global/Insights balances (or hard-blocks settling a real exotic-currency debt)
verdicts.reasoning: settleDebt unconditionally does convertCurrency(amount,currency,'EUR') (actions.ts:115) which for a no-rate currency like ARS — confirmed in _ALLOWED_CURRENCIES (validators.py:73) but absent from static CONVERSION_RATES (17 codes, no ARS) and from the ECB/Frankfurter live feed — falls through _rateFor to 1/1 and returns the raw foreign amount as euroValue; it always spreads that into the POST so the server reject gate (`rate is None AND euro_value is None`, settlements.py:224) never fires and compute_euro_value's cold path returns the client hint verbatim (fx_rates.py:228), while ManualTab.tsx:108 has the correct needsManualEuro=!hasRate gate the settle modal and one-click Settle button (SettlementView.tsx:302/310) lack; P1 not P0 because it requires an exotic no-rate currency.

verdicts.title: Settlement History groups rows by UTC calendar date — evening settlements show under the wrong day for non-UTC users, and same-day rows have unstable ordering
verdicts.reasoning: collectSettlementHistory sets date:(s.createdAt||'').slice(0,10) (viewData.ts:178) off created_at which is DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP=UTC (baseline.py:45), and the History tab labels Today/Yesterday by comparing to new Date().toISOString().slice(0,10) (also UTC, SettlementView.tsx:370) and sorts on the time-stripped date (viewData.ts:184) so same-day rows can't be ordered — real but purely cosmetic display drift.

verdicts.title: Hardcoded English 'Couldn't undo' toast on settlement-delete failure (i18n gap)
verdicts.reasoning: deleteSettlement's failure branch shows a raw literal `Couldn't undo: ${result.error || 'Network error'}` (actions.ts:261) bypassing t(), whereas the localized sibling settlement.toastSettlementFailed exists (en.ts:651) and is used on the create path (actions.ts:177) — a genuine but minor untranslated-string gap on an error toast.
